title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Chickering calls Miss Muggleton from an orchard to meet with the police. Miss Muggleton arrives appearing breathless and disheveled, asking if the officers are from Scotland Yard. Superintendent Corradino questions her, and she provides an alibi, stating she was admiring chrysanthemums during the crime. She describes the chaos of the event, noting she heard a voice command people to "raise your hands," and she believed a refugee girl was being murdered in the dark. Following this, Miss Quincannon suggests the inspectors visit the vicar, Mrs. Harmon, noting that despite her muddled appearance, she is an astute woman.

Inspector Corradino visits Mrs. Harmon at her shabby house. Mrs. Harmon explains that she closed her eyes during the gunshots because she dislikes feeling dizzy. She describes hearing various people screaming and pushing in the dark, and notes that once the lights were restored, she saw a dead, pink-faced foreigner lying on the ground with a revolver by his side.

Later, Corradino and Ridestad review interview records. Ridestad notes that the deceased, Rudi Scherz, had a criminal record involving forgery and con artistry. Corradino expresses doubt regarding the official conclusion that the death was an accident or a suicide, noting that the motive remains unconvincing. Corradino suspects the foreign girl may have conspired with Scherz to steal valuables, though Miss Blacklock and others claim nothing of value is in the house. Corradino also mentions that Miss Bonner believes Scherz attempted to murder Miss Blacklock, though he characterizes her as an unreliable witness whose theory is based solely on her own impressions.
